real_insight: The visible middle of any codebase — the part you can grep for — is the smallest of the three phases, and treating it as the only real phase is a perceptual artifact of standing inside the implementation rather than outside the lifecycle.
objection: Could degrade into 'do not worry about anything you cannot grep for,' but the falsifiability clause explicitly handles that ('the verse's argument depends on phase three actually existing'). The notifications-service example is concrete enough — Linear ticket, Notion PRD, Datadog dashboard, Sentry alert, on-call runbook, migration document — that the three phases are operationally trackable rather than philosophically asserted.
